Signs and Symptoms: Acute Respiratory Infection (ARI)
Treatment: Cough accompanied by short rapid breathing, fever, difficulty of breathing
Prevention and Control: If antibiotic is needed, health workers must be consulted. Supportive measures include increased oral fluids, continued feeding, and anti-pyretics.

Signs and Symptoms: Fever, headache, fatigue, muscle or joint pains, lack of appetite, runny nose, sore throat, cough, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting
Treatment: Oseltamivir or zanamivir are recommended drugs (consult doctor first) . If flu-like symptoms occur, consult doctor immediately.
Prevention and Control: Cover nose and mouth when coughing/sneezing. Wash hands regularly. Avoid touching eyes, nose, or mouth. Increase body resistance (sleep, exercise, stress management, fluids, nutritious food) . Practice social distancing.

Signs and Symptoms: Fever, body weakness or muscle pain, cough, sore throat, sore eyes, diarrhea, difficulty of breathing in severe cases
Treatment: Antiviral drugs, such as oseltamivir, stop the progress of illness if given within the first 2 days of fever.
Prevention and Control: Wash hands thoroughly after handling chicken. Cook chicken thoroughly. Do not catch or keep wild birds . Do not handle sick/dead birds with bare hands. Use gloves/masks.

Signs and Symptoms: Abdominal pain, chronic diarrhea (>2 weeks), loss of appetite, weight loss, vomiting, swelling of the body, muscle wasting
Treatment: Mebendazole 400 mg/day for 20-30 days . Albendazole is also effective.
Prevention and Control: Cook fish thoroughly. Avoid eating raw fish. Use sanitary toilet for waste disposal.

Signs and Symptoms: Appearance of reddish skin lesions which become blisters, weakness, muscle and joint pains, sudden onset of fever
Treatment: Rashes disappear within 1-2 weeks without treatment. Sponge bath/cold compress for fever. Warm shower daily (do not rub lesions). Plenty of fluids.
Prevention and Control: Avoid crowded areas. Isolate patient. Vaccine is available.

Signs and Symptoms: Sudden onset of frequent painless watery stools, vomiting, rapid dehydration
Treatment: Replace lost body fluid with Oral Rehydration Solution (ORESOL) or homemade solution. If diarrhea persists, consult health workers.
Prevention and Control: Drink safe/clean water (boil/chlorinate if unsure) . Cover food. Wash/cook food properly. Dispose waste properly. Clean toilet daily and wash hands . Oral cholera vaccine.

Signs and Symptoms: Sudden high fever (2-7 days), joint/muscle pain, pain behind eyes, weakness, skin rashes, nosebleeding, abdominal pain, vomiting coffee-colored matter, dark stools, difficulty breathing
Treatment: Do not give aspirin. Rehydrate. If fever/symptoms persist for 2+ days, bring to hospital.
Prevention and Control: Search and destroy breeding sites (cover drums, replace vase water, clean gutters, dispose cans/tires) . Wear long pants/sleeves. Use mosquito repellant. Seek early consultation. Fogging if impending outbreak.

Signs and Symptoms: Passage of watery stools at least 3 times a day, excessive thirst, sunken eyeballs, sunken fontanel in infants
Treatment: Give Oral Rehydration Solution (ORESOL) or rice soup. Continued feeding is important.
Prevention and Control: Drink safe water. Eat well-cooked food. Avoid street-vended food. Cover food. Wash fruits/vegetables. Use toilet. Wash hands.

Signs and Symptoms: Sore throat, hoarseness, painful swallowing, swollen neck glands, thick gray membrane on throat/tonsils, difficulty breathing, nasal discharge, fever, chills, malaise
Treatment: Antitoxin injected into vein or muscle. Antibiotics such as penicillin or erythromycin.
Prevention and Control: Immunize infants with three doses of DPT.

Signs and Symptoms: Sudden onset of fever, intense weakness, muscle pain, headache, sore throat, vomiting, diarrhea, rash, impaired kidney/liver function, internal/external bleeding
Treatment: No standard treatment.
Prevention and Control: Wear protective clothing (masks, gloves, gowns, goggles). Use infection-control measures and isolation. Avoid contact with blood/secretions.

Signs and Symptoms: Pain and swelling of breast, vagina, scrotum, legs, and arms; fever, cough, chills, wheezing
Treatment: Selective treatment with DEC (Diethylcarbamazine Citrate) . Mass treatment with DEC and albendazole .
Prevention and Control: Wear long-sleeved shirt and long pants in endemic areas. Sleep under mosquito net or use repellant.

Signs and Symptoms: Fever, sore throat, malaise, painful red blister-like lesions on tongue/gums/cheeks, red rash on palms/soles/buttocks, irritability in infants, loss of appetite
Treatment: Treatment is directed toward relief of symptoms (fever/sore throat). Topical oral anesthetic for mouth sores. Over-the-counter pain medications (acetaminophen/ibuprofen).
Prevention and Control: Good hygiene (proper handwashing). Disinfect premises and infected materials.

Signs and Symptoms: Fever, flu-like symptoms, vomiting, tiredness, abdominal discomfort, dark/brownish urine, jaundice
Treatment: No specific medicine. Isolation, rest, plenty of fluids, avoid fatty foods. Intravenous fluids if needed.
Prevention and Control: Wash hands. Dispose waste properly. Thoroughly cook shellfish. Safe food handling. Hepatitis A vaccine.

Signs and Symptoms: Weakness, stomach upset, dark urine or very pale stools, jaundice
Treatment: None.
Prevention and Control: Immunization of infants (3 doses). Hepatitis B immunoglobulin for newborns of positive mothers. Use safe/clean injection equipment. Vaccination of adults in high-risk settings. Use condoms.

Signs and Symptoms: Headache, nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, jaundice, weakness, fatigue, dark yellow urine, light-colored stools, yellowish eyes
Treatment: Medicines such as interferon and ribavirin (do not cure but make people feel better).
Prevention and Control: Use condoms. Do not share injecting devices. Wear gloves if touching blood. Do not share toothbrush/razor. Ensure clean tools for tattooing.

Signs and Symptoms: Early: fever, fatigue, swollen lymph nodes, diarrhea, weight loss, cough, shortness of breath. Primary: fever, muscle soreness, rash, headache, sore throat, mouth/genital ulcers, swollen glands, joint pain, night sweats
Treatment: No cure; medications can slow down progression.
Prevention and Control: Sexual intercourse within monogamous relationship. Use male latex condoms. Use clean needles.

Signs and Symptoms: Fever of at least 38C, headache, runny nose, sore throat, cough, muscle or joint pains
Treatment: Antiviral agents (oseltamivir or zanamivir) within first 2 days. Rest, fluids, nutritious foods . Paracetamol for fever (no aspirin for children). Antibiotics only for complications.
Prevention and Control: Influenza vaccination. Minimize contact with sick persons. Avoid crowds. Distance at least 1 meter. Cover mouth/nose. Wash hands.

Signs and Symptoms: Flu-like illness (fever, chills, tiredness, headache, nausea, vomiting), confusion and agitation in early stage
Treatment: No specific treatment; treat symptoms and complications.
Prevention and Control: Get vaccinated. Avoid mosquito bites. Minimize outdoor activities at dusk/dawn. Use repellant, screens, mosquito nets .

Signs and Symptoms: High persistent fever, reddening of whites of eyes, red mucus membranes of mouth, red cracked lips, strawberry tongue, swollen lymph nodes, skin rashes
Treatment: Intravenous gamma globulin. Consult doctor if high fever persists >24 hours.
Prevention and Control: No known measures to prevent this disorder.

Signs and Symptoms: Skin patch with loss of sensation (reddish or copper-colored), enlarged peripheral nerve, positive slit-skin smear
Treatment: Multidrug therapy (MDT) using rifampicin, clofazimine, and dapsone .
Prevention and Control: Treat all cases. Avoid direct contact with untreated patients. Personal hygiene, good nutrition, rest, exercise, clean environment .

Signs and Symptoms: Fever, muscle pain, calf-muscle pain, reddish eyes, headache. Severe: yellowish discoloration, dark urine, light stools, low urine output
Treatment: Antibiotics prescribed by physician. Early treatment within 2 days prevents complications.
Prevention and Control: Avoid swimming in contaminated/flood water. Use boots and gloves. Drain contaminated water. Control rodents.

Signs and Symptoms: Insomnia, depression, confusion, personality changes, memory problems. Progression: dementia, jerky movements, worsening sight, muscle weakness. Final: paralysis, coma, death
Treatment: No effective treatment available.
Prevention and Control: Do not eat meat/meat products from countries with reported BSE cases.

Signs and Symptoms: Chills, high-grade fever, severe headache, vomiting
Treatment: Artemether-Lumefantrine + Primaquine; Quinine Sulfate + Doxycycline/Tetracycline/Clindamycin; Chloroquine + Primaquine .
Prevention and Control: Use insecticidal mosquito nets. Wear long sleeves/pants. Use repellants/screens. Clear hanging branches along streams. Blood exam for symptoms.

Signs and Symptoms: Cough, runny nose, red eyes/conjunctivitis, fever, blotchy rash (>3 days)
Treatment: Nutritional support and oral rehydration . Antibiotics for infections. Vitamin A supplementation.
Prevention and Control: Immunize infants with measles vaccine at 9 months. Vitamin A supplementation during vaccination.

Signs and Symptoms: Fever, cough, sore throat, pinpoint rashes becoming bruise-like patches, severe skin lesions, unstable vital signs, meningitis signs (stiff neck, convulsion)
Treatment: Prompt treatment with penicillin. Consult doctor immediately.
Prevention and Control: Immunization with meningococcal vaccine. Avoid crowds/close contact . Healthy lifestyle. Clean environment. Do not share utensils. Wash hands.

Signs and Symptoms: Dry cough, blood stained or rust-colored sputum, chest/back pain, PTB-like signs
Treatment: Praziquantel.
Prevention and Control: Cook crabs thoroughly. Dispose sputum/waste properly. Control snail population. Treat cases.

Signs and Symptoms: Cough persisting 2 or more weeks, fits of coughing, cough followed by vomiting
Treatment: Antibiotics.
Prevention and Control: Three primary immunization doses of DTaP with booster doses .

Signs and Symptoms: Fever, severe muscle pain, paralysis, difficulty breathing, inability to move affected arm/leg
Treatment: No treatment; support treatment based on symptoms.
Prevention and Control: Vaccination (OPV or IPV). Personal hygiene. Improve public sanitation.

Signs and Symptoms: Headache, fever, pain/numbness of bite, delirium, paralysis, muscle spasms, hydrophobia, aerophobia
Treatment: Wash wound immediately. Consult physician. Observe dog for 14 days. Post-exposure vaccination if needed.
Prevention and Control: Immunize pets. Keep pets from roaming. Pre-exposure vaccine for high-risk individuals.

Signs and Symptoms: High fever (>38C), headache, discomfort, body aches, dry cough, difficulty breathing
Treatment: No specific cure; refer to DOH SARS Referral Hospital .
Prevention and Control: Health screening in airports/seaports. Quarantine contacts/new arrivals.

Signs and Symptoms: Abdominal pain, low-grade fever, loose bowel movement, bloody stool, liver inflammation, bulging abdomen, spleen enlargement
Treatment: Praziquantel.
Prevention and Control: Submit stool for exam. Use sanitary toilets. Avoid infested waters. Fence animals. Clean environment.

Signs and Symptoms: Soreness, unusual lumps/sores, itching, pain when urinating, unusual discharge (symptoms vary)
Treatment: Creams (yeast/balano-posthitis). Antibiotics (chlamydia, gonorrhea, syphilis, trichomoniasis). Antivirals (herpes, HIV). Lotions/liquid nitrogen (HPV). Oral meds/injections (Hep B).
Prevention and Control: Abstinence. Monogamous relationship. Use condoms.

Signs and Symptoms: Eyelids stuck together, pain upon exposure to sunlight/foreign-body solution, redness, inflammation
Treatment: No specific treatment during acute phase. Consult ophthalmologist if profuse discharge or blurring.
Prevention and Control: Wash hands frequently. Minimize hand-to-eye contact. Use own towels/glasses. Disinfect surfaces. Wash clothes/towels.

Signs and Symptoms: Cough for two weeks or more, fever, chest/back pain, weight loss, blood-streaked sputum
Treatment: INH (latent TB). INH, rifampin, ethambutol, pyrazinamide, streptomycin (active TB) .
Prevention and Control: DOTS treatment. Healthy lifestyle. Ventilation. Hygiene. Cover cough.

Signs and Symptoms: Sustained high fever, headache, malaise, anorexia, diarrhea or constipation, abdominal discomfort
Treatment: Antibiotics (ciprofloxacin, ceftriaxone).
Prevention and Control: Avoid untreated water/boil water. Cook and cover food. Avoid street-vended foods. Wash hands. Clean surroundings. Immunization.

Signs and Symptoms: Memory loss, difficulty finding words, difficulty understanding, difficulty performing routine tasks, personality and mood changes
Treatment: No cure. Supportive medical care, pharmacological treatment for symptoms, emotional support .
Prevention and Control: Eat right, exercise, stay mentally/socially active, keep stress in check.

Signs and Symptoms: Fatigue, unexplained weight loss, fever, weakness, pain, skin changes
Treatment: Specific for each type of cancer.
Prevention and Control: Quit smoking. Limit alcohol. Watch diet. Regular consults. Physical activity. Immunization (Hep B, HPV).

Signs and Symptoms: Extreme bone pain (esp at night), swelling/tenderness, fracture, fatigue, weight loss
Treatment: Amputation, limb-sparing surgery, orthopedic treatment, bioengineering, radiotherapy, chemotherapy .
Prevention and Control: No specific prevention. X-ray for persistent pain. Screening.

Signs and Symptoms: Lump/swelling, change in size/shape, one breast lower, puckering/dimpling, nipple retraction, sore in nipple
Treatment: Surgery, radiation, hormone therapy, chemotherapy, targeted therapy.
Prevention and Control: Healthy diet, exercise, maintain weight, mammography, Breast Self-Examination (BSE) .

Signs and Symptoms: Prolonged fever/illness, pallor, bruising, localized pain/limping, masses/swelling, frequent headaches/vomiting, eye changes, weight loss
Treatment: Surgery, radiotherapy, chemotherapy.
Prevention and Control: Regular medical check-up.

Signs and Symptoms: Unusual bleeding, unpleasant vaginal discharge (often asymptomatic)
Treatment: Concurrent chemotherapy and complete radiotherapy.
Prevention and Control: Pap smear. One-partner relationship. Delay first intercourse. Condoms. HPV vaccine.

Signs and Symptoms: Change in bowel habits, abdominal discomfort, weight loss, unexplained anemia, blood in stool
Treatment: Surgery.
Prevention and Control: Removal of polyps. Barium enema or colonoscopy.

Signs and Symptoms: Fatigue, weakness, bone pain, weight loss, bruising, red spots, nosebleed, recurring infections, swollen nodes/liver/spleen, sweating
Treatment: Chemotherapy.
Prevention and Control: Avoid risk factors (radiation/chemicals).

Signs and Symptoms: Abdominal pain, weight loss, weakness, loss of appetite, abdominal mass/enlarged liver
Treatment: Surgery (early stage), palliative care .
Prevention and Control: Hepatitis B vaccination. Improve sanitation.

Signs and Symptoms: Persistent dry cough, chest pain, blood-stained sputum, shortness of breath/wheezing, pneumonia/bronchitis, swelling of neck/face, weight loss, fatigue, difficulty swallowing
Treatment: Surgery, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted drug therapy .
Prevention and Control: Stop smoking. Avoid secondhand smoke. Healthy diet/exercise .

Signs and Symptoms: Painless swelling of lymph nodes, fatigue, fever, chills, night sweats, weight loss, coughing, itching, pain after alcohol
Treatment: Chemotherapy, radiotherapy, bone marrow transplant, stem cell transplant .
Prevention and Control: Avoid known risk factors.

Signs and Symptoms: Abdominal mass, mass felt during pelvic examination (often asymptomatic)
Treatment: Surgery, chemotherapy, palliative care .
Prevention and Control: Birth control pills. Annual pelvic examinations.

Signs and Symptoms: Trouble urinating, decreased urine stream, blood in urine/semen, leg swelling, pelvic discomfort, bone pain
Treatment: Radiation therapy, hormone therapy, chemotherapy, radical prostatectomy .
Prevention and Control: Balanced diet, quit smoking, exercise, avoid too much alcohol, regular check-up .

Signs and Symptoms: Change in bowel habits, transanal bleeding, weight loss, anemia, blood in stool
Treatment: Surgery, palliative care.
Prevention and Control: Rectal examination/proctoscopy. Polyp removal.

Signs and Symptoms: Early: indigestion, dyspepsia, loss of appetite, anemia. Advanced: weight loss, difficulty swallowing, vomiting, abdominal mass
Treatment: Surgery.
Prevention and Control: Upper gastrointestinal series or endoscopy .

Signs and Symptoms: Hard mass in neck, nodules, rapid goiter enlargement, lymph node enlargement, hoarseness, difficulty swallowing/breathing
Treatment: Surgery, radioactive iodine .
Prevention and Control: Fine needle aspiration biopsy of nodules.

Signs and Symptoms: Loss of awareness, movement/sensation/mood disturbances, fractures, bruising
Treatment: Anti-epileptic medication.
Prevention and Control: Avoid head trauma and birth anoxia.

Signs and Symptoms: Pain and stiffness in joints, swelling, warmth, creaking
Treatment: No specific treatment to halt degeneration.
Prevention and Control: Reduce weight. Avoid excessive stress on joint cartilage.

Signs and Symptoms: No signs until bone fractures
Treatment: Medications to stop bone loss (alendronate, etc.) and increase bone formation (teriparatide) .
Prevention and Control: Change unhealthy lifestyle habits (e.g. quit smoking).

Signs and Symptoms: Numbness/weakness of face/arm/leg, confusion, difficulty speaking, dizziness, vomiting, difficulty walking, visual difficulty, severe headache
Treatment: Immediate emergency room treatment. CT scan. Physical and speech therapy.
Prevention and Control: Healthy diet, physical activity, healthy weight, control BP/sugar, quit smoking, moderate alcohol, avoid stress .

Signs and Symptoms: Chronic cough with phlegm, breathlessness, wheezing
Treatment: Medicines to dilate air passages (not curable).
Prevention and Control: Quit smoking. Avoid secondhand smoke/pollutants . Vaccinations.

Signs and Symptoms: Cardiac arrest, chest pain/discomfort, shortness of breath, palpitations, irregular heartbeat, dizziness/fainting
Treatment: Lowering cholesterol/BP/diabetes control, stents .
Prevention and Control: Healthy diet, physical activity, lower fat/cholesterol, control BP/diabetes, quit smoking, lower alcohol .

Signs and Symptoms: Warm flushed skin, faintness, dizziness, weakness, headache, high fever, rapid heartbeat, convulsion, unconsciousness
Treatment: Move to shade, elevate legs, sip cool water, remove clothing, apply water/ice packs, bring to hospital immediately .
Prevention and Control: Limit outdoor time, drink water, avoid tea/coffee/alcohol, wear hat/long sleeves, schedule activities when cooler .

Signs and Symptoms: Often none. Headache, dizziness, blurred vision, nausea, chest pain, shortness of breath
Treatment: Anti-hypertensive medications.
Prevention and Control: Nutritious low-salt/low-fat diet, exercise, healthy weight, moderate alcohol, quit smoking, reduce stress .

Signs and Symptoms: Increased weight, thickness around midsection, fat deposits, large frame, difficulty in daily activities, lethargy, breathlessness
Treatment: Limit high-calorie/sugar intake, eat fruits/vegetables, physical activity .
Prevention and Control: Achieve energy balance and healthy weight.

Signs and Symptoms: Fatigue, weight loss, thirst, urination, eating, poor wound healing, blurry vision, infections, irritability
Treatment: Oral hypoglycemic medications, insulin .
Prevention and Control: Healthy diet, exercise, moderate alcohol .

Signs and Symptoms: Weight loss/gain, sleep difficulty, fatigue, agitation/slowness, guilt, diminished concentration, suicidal thoughts
Treatment: Antidepressant medications, psychological interventions (therapy) .
Prevention and Control: (Not explicitly stated in text).

Signs and Symptoms: Smell of alcohol, flushed skin, bloodshot eyes, appearance deterioration, attention/memory loss, desire to drink, withdrawal
Treatment: Community settings, voluntary mutual help organizations, medical/familial interventions .
Prevention and Control: Parental supervision, alcohol control policies, substitute activities .

Signs and Symptoms: Nausea, chills, teeth clenching, cramped muscles, blurred vision, floating sensations, irrational behavior, hallucinations, excitement, confusion, sweating, dry mouth, dilated pupils
Treatment: Drug treatment, abstinence, detoxification, counseling .
Prevention and Control: Parental supervision, control policies, avoid situations encouraging use, healthier activities .

Signs and Symptoms: Goiter
Treatment: Use iodized salt, eat iodine-rich foods, iodine supplements .
Prevention and Control: (Same as treatment).

Signs and Symptoms: Weakness, insomnia, tired easily, lack of appetite, lack of concentration, pallor
Treatment: Eat iron-rich foods, iron supplements with folic acid, deworming, breastfeeding, increase vitamin C .
Prevention and Control: (Same as treatment).

Signs and Symptoms: Night blindness, Bitot's spot, dry/hazy cornea, crater-like defect, softened cornea, xerophthalmia
Treatment: Daily oral vitamin A supplements, vitamin A-rich foods .
Prevention and Control: Breastfeeding, vitamin A-rich diet, vitamin A capsules .

Signs and Symptoms: Lockjaw, stiffness of neck, difficulty swallowing, muscle spasm, fever, inability to suck
Treatment: Antibiotics, antitoxin drugs, sedatives.
Prevention and Control: Immunize women with tetanus toxoid, clean delivery/cord care, immunize infants with DPT .

Signs and Symptoms: Delayed sitting/crawling/walking/talking, memory problems, trouble understanding rules/consequences, trouble solving problems
Treatment: Enriched and supportive environment.
Prevention and Control: (Not explicitly stated).

Signs and Symptoms: Swelling or redness of skin, center liquefies as pus collects
Treatment: Do not squeeze, apply warm compress, cover boil, drainage/antibiotics if needed .
Prevention and Control: Wash hands frequently, take a bath daily .

Signs and Symptoms: Painful red skin, blistering, swelling, fever, peeling
Treatment: Cool shower/compress, dry bandages, ibuprofen, moisturizing cream .
Prevention and Control: Protective clothing, sunscreen.

Signs and Symptoms: Prickly sensation, rash (tiny bumps)
Treatment: Cleanse skin, apply corn starch/baking soda, consult if infected.
Prevention and Control: Bath daily, limit sun, dry sweat, loose clothing .

Signs and Symptoms: Diarrhea, food poisoning
Treatment: (Not explicitly stated).
Prevention and Control: Avoid buying meat with pale color, foul smell, sticky consistency, cold/frozen, lower price .

Signs and Symptoms: Numbness, dizziness, pricking sensation/paralysis, weakness, rapid pulse, difficulty talking/swallowing/breathing, headache, abdominal pain, vomiting, diarrhea
Treatment: Ventilatory support, fluid therapy, detoxification.
Prevention and Control: Do not eat shellfish/alamang during red tide, wash/remove gills/intestines of fish/squids/crabs .

Signs and Symptoms: Burns, throat pain, garlic odor, nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, shock, perforation
Treatment: DO NOT induce vomiting, give egg whites, bring to hospital, bathe if dermal exposure .
Prevention and Control: Keep out of children's reach.

Signs and Symptoms: Burns, vomiting
Treatment: Swallowed: DO NOT induce vomiting, egg whites, hospital. Eye: wash 15 mins. Skin: wash water. Inhaled: fresh air .
Prevention and Control: (Implicit prevention by avoidance).
